plays.story: Pathfinder is ending its relationship with Archives of Nethys, cutting off the SRD reference tool millions of GMs rely on

angles.bizzal-games: Title: 'Pathfinder Just Pulled the Rug on Archives of Nethys — Here's Your Backup Plan.' Practical survival guide for GMs and players: what breaks at the table tonight, which mirrors/alternatives to bookmark, and how to keep prepping sessions without AoN's search.
angles.itsalreadywritten: Title: 'When the SRD Goes Dark: What Pathfinder's AoN Split Means for Open Gaming Content.' Dig into the licensing/business logic behind the split and what it signals for third-party publishers who built businesses on that 'open' promise.

plays.story: Beadle & Grimm's Deadfall boxed set launches at a jaw-dropping $500 price point with minis, posters, and bonus adventures

angles.bizzal-games: Title: 'Is Beadle & Grimm's $500 Deadfall Set Worth It? A Table-Ready Breakdown.' Practical value analysis for GMs deciding whether to buy in — what actually gets used at the table vs. shelf candy.
angles.itsalreadywritten: Title: 'The $500 Adventure: How TTRPG Publishing Went Luxury.' Editorial piece on the trend of premium box-set storytelling — what it means for how adventures get written, packaged, and monetized when the book itself becomes the collectible.

plays.story: EU sanctions 'Stern,' likely the most prolific ransomware operator ever, in a Chainalysis-backed enforcement action tied to billions in damages

angles.itsalreadypriced: Title: 'How Blockchain Forensics Took Down History's Biggest Ransomware Kingpin.' Follow the money: how on-chain tracing turned ransom payments into a sanctions case, and what it means for crypto's role in illicit finance narratives.
angles.itsalreadywhen: Title: 'Stern Is Sanctioned — But Is Ransomware Actually Slowing Down?' Pair the Stern takedown with Dark Reading's finding that identity attacks now outpace exploits as the top ransomware cause — what defenders should actually prioritize.

plays.story: Ostium pauses trading after a multimillion-dollar oracle exploit, reigniting debate over whether AI is fueling a DeFi 'hackpocalypse'

angles.itsalreadypriced: Title: 'Ostium's Oracle Just Got Drained — What Every DeFi Trader Needs to Know.' Break down the exploit mechanics, market fallout, and why oracle risk keeps blindsiding traders who think they've done their diligence.
angles.itsalreadywhen: Title: 'AI Didn't Cause the DeFi Hackpocalypse — Bad Oracles Did.' Use the Ostium exploit as a case study against the Dragonfly-fueled AI panic narrative, refocusing on the boring infrastructure flaws that actually get exploited.
